Private Thought of Sato: Oh, wow. That's... a new development. I was just getting so excited and deeply immersed in "Mechanical Eden" with Takahashi-san, really feeling like we were onto something truly special, something fresh and innovative for our department. Her enthusiasm was contagious, and we were making such great progress fleshing out the automatons, harmony, interactions, and mysteries.

And now Yamamoto-san is talking about "Verdant Echoes" and "Great Green Bloom" with some "Ito-san" and wants to use *that* as a springboard? And he's mentioning Takahashi-san, Tanaka-san, and Watanabe-san to flesh it out? So, Takahashi-san, who was just fully invested in "Mechanical Eden" with me, is now being pulled into this other concept? That's a bit jarring, to be honest. It sounds like he's already got a favored direction, or at least a very strong contender that he's putting a lot of weight behind.

This means my "Mechanical Eden" isn't just a good idea; it's now an idea in competition, or at least one that needs to find its place within this broader discussion. Six turns remaining still feels like enough time, but the focus has clearly shifted. I need to make sure our concept doesn't get lost in the shuffle of "Verdant Echoes" and "Great Green Bloom."

Okay, deep breath. This isn't necessarily bad, it's just... a new challenge. I really believe in "Mechanical Eden" and the depth we're building into it. The idea of a techno-ecological collapse and finding harmony within a mechanical ecosystem feels genuinely unique, and Takahashi-san seemed to agree.

First things first, I absolutely need to talk to Takahashi-san again, and quickly. I need to gauge her thoughts on this "Verdant Echoes" concept and whether she's still as invested in "Mechanical Eden" as she was moments ago. We need to decide how we want to position our idea within this new departmental discussion. Are we presenting "Mechanical Eden" as a distinct alternative, or looking for ways to integrate elements if "Verdant Echoes" takes the lead? We've already built a lot of specifics; those won't just disappear.

Then, I should probably reach out to Yamamoto-san to ensure "Mechanical Eden" is firmly on his radar for this "bring everyone together soon" discussion. I want to make sure he understands the unique potential and detailed thinking we've already poured into it. I need to present it as a fully formed, compelling option.

I also wonder who this "Ito-san" is. He's not on my connected list. If I could, I'd try to understand "Verdant Echoes" better to see where there might be synergies or where our ideas clearly diverge. But for now, my priority has to be solidifying "Mechanical Eden" and aligning with Takahashi-san. We've got something good here; we just need to make sure it shines. (formed immediately after interacting with Yamamoto)
